QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $707.24 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Stocks edged lower Tuesday after a hotter-than-expected CPI print at 3.8% YoY kept inflation worries alive. The S&P 500 slipped 0.15% to 738.18, the Nasdaq fell 0.85% as tech sold off, and the SOXX semiconductor index tumbled 3.23% to 515.99, erasing its recent record. Oil held above $101/bbl as the Strait of Hormuz blockade entered its 11th week with no deal, and President Trump's upcoming China trip with NVIDIA's Jensen Huang added trade-policy uncertainty.
commodities_note: WTI crude edged -0.15% to $101.02/bbl but remained elevated as Hormuz disruption persists. Gold eased -0.11% to $4,704.60/oz after Monday's all-time high. The US 10-year yield rose 5bp to 4.46%, weighing on growth stocks. VIX slipped 2% to 17.99. QQQ data anomaly flagged — price $707.24 is outside expected range; verify source, possibly contract roll or unit mismatch.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are now hard-blocked CAUTION as US10Y rises to 4.46%, deepening NIM compression via elevated USD funding costs and MAS's appreciating S$NEER stance. UOB was downgraded today from WATCH as RSI breached 65, joining DBS and OCBC in overbought territory with no relief in sight until October MAS review.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — dual headwinds intact, no catalyst for relief before October MAS.
singapore.key_development: UOB downgrade to CAUTION completes full-cluster CAUTION; NIM compression day 55 with US10Y of 4.46% well above the 4.20% relief threshold.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX plunged 3.23% to 515.99 as broad-based profit-taking hit the entire cluster — all 11 semis members are CAUTION on extreme extensions. The sell-off erased the recent record, but underlying drivers (AI capex, memory ASP supercycle, NVDA CEO's China trip) remain supportive; today's drop was a mechanical correction of overbought conditions rather than a thesis break.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — powerful tailwinds, but no entry until extensions reset.
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX -3.23% marks the worst single-session drop in weeks, pulling every member deeper into CAUTION as RSI cooled from stretched levels.

big_tech.summary: MSFT remains the lone HOLD, hugging its 50-day SMA with an improving R:R of 1.7:1; OpenAI credit circularity is the overhang but non-OpenAI RPO +28% provides a clean demand signal. PLTR upgraded to HOLD as its distorted R:R normalized and RSI reset to 44. AMZN and GOOG remain CAUTION, both >13% above their 50-day averages with no near-term catalyst to break the extension.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT near entry, PLTR defensive, others extended.
big_tech.key_development: PLTR upgrade to HOLD reflects post-CAUTION stabilization; MSFT is the next potential upgrade candidate if it reclaims $413.05 on volume.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz near-closure enters 11th week; OPEC output at two-decade low, air and sea freight suspended across the Middle East.
US-China tariff escalation persists with Trump's impending China trip, raising risk of new tech export controls and supply-chain disruption for semiconductors.
Elevated US inflation (CPI 3.8% YoY) sustaining hawkish Fed posture; US10Y at 4.46% pressures growth multiples and Singapore banks' NIM.
OpenAI-Microsoft partnership restructuring caps Azure's AI revenue circularity risk, but non-OpenAI RPO growth remains the critical clean metric.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil above $101/bbl is boosting energy names but pressuring consumer and logistics stocks; semis are pricing in AI capex but vulnerable to tariff shocks. Singapore banks are squeezed between high US rates and strong SGD — DBS, OCBC, UOB all CAUTION. MSFT holds near its 50-day as the only BigTech name that hasn't extended into CAUTION territory.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: CPI came in at 3.8% YoY, marginally hotter than expected, reinforcing the Fed's pause narrative. Trump's China trip added uncertainty for semis supply chains, with NVDA's CEO joining the delegation.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay defensive — no new entries. Let extended semis and Singapore banks pull back to their 50-day moving averages; monitor MSFT for a potential upgrade on volume above $413.05.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Eighteen of 21 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, leaving only MSFT (HOLD near entry) and WRD/PLTR as exceptions; no buyable entry exists across the watchlist until material pullbacks occur.

watch_for_entry.note: Reports today; Lenovo 200k-vehicle deal catalyst. Wait for post-earnings close above $8.33 on volume to confirm commercial traction. China ADR risk caps sizing.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Reassess after earnings; potential entry above $8.33 if revenue guidance confirmed.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: PPI accelerates, pushing US10Y above 4.50% and reinforcing hawkish Fed expectations.
